q: Surface tension is the tendency of the surface of a liquid to behave like a stretched ___ skin (or membrane).
Surface tension is due to the attractive forces between liquid molecules called ___.
Cohesion refers to forces between molecules of the ___ substance.
Cohesion makes water molecules pull together making the water surface to stretch like an ___ skin.
Surface tension is the reason a ___ strider is able to stride along water surface without sinking.
Paperclips, razor blades and light coins are able to rest on water without sinking although their ___ is higher than that of water.
Surface tension is defined as the force per unit length that acts across any line on a surface tending to pull the surface ___.
Surface tension $\gamma = \frac{F}{l}$, where $F$ = force, $l$ = length, $\gamma$ = ___.
The SI unit of surface tension is ___ per metre (N m$^{-1}$).
Surface tension can also be referred to as a force because it is the tension between liquid ___.
Capillary action is defined as the spontaneous flow of a liquid into a ___ tube or porous material.
Capillary action is caused by the combination of cohesive forces of the liquid and the ___ forces between the liquid and tube material.
Adhesion refers to forces between molecules of ___ substances.
These cohesive and adhesive forces pull the liquid into the ___.
In order for wicking to occur, a tube needs to be sufficiently ___ in diameter.
Capillarity (or) capillary action is also the rise or depression of a liquid in a narrow tube of small ___.
The rise of water underneath the ground is an example of ___ action.
Capillary action helps bringing water up into the ___.
When a glass tube with a small inside diameter is dipped into water, the water ___ into the tube.
The smaller the bore of a capillary tube, the ___ the water will rise.
Liquids that rise in small bore tubes inserted into the liquid are said to ___ the tube.
Liquids that are depressed within thin tubes below the surface of the surrounding liquid do not ___ the tube.
Water is a liquid that wets glass capillary tubes; ___ is one that does not.
Capillarity does not occur for ___ liquids.
When a glass tube is dipped into water, the adhesion between glass and water causes a thin film of water to be drawn up over the inner and outer surfaces of the ___.
Since the ___ force is greater than cohesive force, water level rises until there is a sufficient mass of liquid for gravitational forces to overcome these intermolecular forces.
The actual amount of rise depends on the surface tension $\gamma$, the contact angle $\theta$ and the ___ $r$ of the tube.
The height $h$ to which the liquid can be lifted is given by $h=\frac{2\gamma \cos \theta}{\text{___} gr}$.
In the capillary rise formula, $\rho$ is the density of the ___.
